title: Evidence
description: A single piece of evidence supporting a field in an extracted event. Evidence connects extracted claims back to source text with provenance and strength assessment.

evidence_id.description: Unique identifier for this evidence item.

evidence_type.description: How the evidence was derived from the source. direct_quote is verbatim text, paraphrase is restated, quantitative_claim is a specific number or metric, inferred is a logical deduction from surrounding text, contextual is background information that supports the claim.

text.description: The extracted or quoted supporting text from the source.

source_location.description: Best-effort provenance pointing back to where in the source this evidence was found.

url.description: URL of the source document, if available.

paragraph_index.description: Zero-based index of the paragraph within the source document.

char_range.description: Character offset range within the source text.

supports_field.description: Which event field this evidence supports, using dot notation for nested fields (e.g. "event_type", "occurred_at", "metadata.funding_amount").

strength.description: How strongly this evidence supports the claim. strong: unambiguous direct support. moderate: clear but indirect or partially ambiguous. weak: suggestive but not conclusive. circumstantial: tangential or requires significant interpretation.

extraction_note.description: Optional note from the extraction model about difficulty or ambiguity encountered when extracting this evidence.
